item.itunes:author: ayrahikari
item.itunes:summary:
Motorola Smart Connect and Ready For
(Utilities)
Motorola Smart Connect or Ready For (old name) app ported for any roms
Download windows client:
https://apps.microsoft.com/detail/XP8JRF5SXV03ZM

(Utilities)

item.title: Pokete [Arcade]
item.itunes:title: Pokete [Arcade]
item.itunes:author: hb882
item.itunes:summary:
Pokete
(Arcade)
Pokete is a small terminal based game in the style of a very popular and old game by Gamefreak.
NOTE: This is only to provide appimages, I am not the developer
[read more]

(Notes)

item.title: sticker-convert [Chat & Messenging]
item.itunes:title: sticker-convert [Chat & Messenging]
item.itunes:author: hb882
item.itunes:summary:
sticker-convert
(Chat & Messenging)
Convert (animated) stickers to/from WhatsApp, Telegram, Signal, Line, Kakao, Viber, iMessage. Written in Python.
NOTE: This is only to provide appimages, I am not the developer
[read more]

(Chat & Messenging)

[read more]

sticker-convert
(Chat & Messenging)

item.title: Multi Agent Suite for ROS [Utilities]
item.itunes:title: Multi Agent Suite for ROS [Utilities]
item.itunes:author: hb882
item.itunes:summary:
Multi Agent Suite for ROS
(Utilities)
Based on the FKIE Multimaster, this suite provides a collection of packages for ROS 1 and 2 for discovering, synchronizing (ROS 1), monitoring, and managing nodes on different hosts using a graphical user interface.
NOTE: This is only to provide appimages, I am not the developer
[read more]
item.description:
Multi Agent Suite for ROS
(Utilities)
Based on the FKIE Multimaster, this suite provides a collection of packages for ROS 1 and 2 for discovering, synchronizing (ROS 1), monitoring, and managing nodes on different hosts using a graphical user interface.
NOTE: This is only to provide appimages, I am not the developer
[read more]
item.content:encoded:
Multi Agent Suite for ROS
(Utilities)
Based on the FKIE Multimaster, this suite provides a collection of packages for ROS 1 and 2 for discovering, synchronizing (ROS 1), monitoring, and managing nodes on different hosts using a graphical user interface.
NOTE: This is only to provide appimages, I am not the developer
[read more]

item.title: digiKam [Graphic Apps]
item.itunes:title: digiKam [Graphic Apps]
item.itunes:author: digikamdeveloper
item.itunes:summary:
digiKam
(Graphic Apps)
digiKam is an advanced digital [b]photo management application[/b], which makes importing and organizing digital photos a "snap". The photos can be organized in albums which can be sorted chronologically, by directory layout or by custom collections.
Tired of the directory constraints? Don’t worry, digiKam also provides [b]tagging functionality[/b]. You tag your images which can be spread out across multiple folders and digiKam provides fast and intuitive ways to browse these tagged images. You can also add [b]comments and rating[/b] to your images. digiKam makes use of a fast and robust database to store these meta-informations which makes adding and editing of comments and tags very reliable.

An easy to use interface is provided that enables you to [b]connect to your camera[/b] and preview, download and/or delete your images. digiKam includes too an image editor for image corrections and manipulations.

Another tool, which most artists and photographers will be familiar with, is a [b]Light Table[/b]. This tool assists artists and photographers with reviewing their work ensuring only the highest quality. A light table will show the artist their imperfections in which they can go and touch up. Well in digiKam, the light table function provides the user a similar experience. You can import a photo, drag it onto the light table, and touch up only the areas that need it.

The digiKam [b]Image Editor[/b] come with some common tools e.g. red eye correction or colors correction. Additional tools are provided with the main application to process advanced corrections on image like color management, noise reduction, or special effects. digiKam Image Editor support all camera RAW file formats, 16 bits color depth, Exif/Makernote/IPTC/GPS metadata, Color management, tagging/rating/comments pictures, etc.

A lots of search tools are also available to be able to find specific photo on whole collection : [b]Time line[/b], Calendar, Advanced Search using multiple and complex database queries (tags, rating, keywords, date, location, camera, shot settings, etc.)

A stand-alone image editor version named [b]Showfoto[/b] is also available. It runs without digiKam images database support, but provides all Image Editor tools.

See the related project page for more information:

[url]http://www.digikam.org[/url]

Online documentations:

[url]http://docs.kde.org/development/en/extragear-graphics/digikam/index.html[/url]
[url]http://docs.kde.org/development/en/extragear-graphics/showfoto/index.html[/url]

NOTE:

- Please, use the digiKam mailing-list for all comments:

[b]digikam-users at kde dot org[/b]

- Please, use Bug Tracking System for wishes and reports:
[url]http://bugs.kde.org/buglist.cgi?product=digikam[/url]

You can contact developers on Freenode IRC channels:

[b]#digikam[/b]

or using the developers mailing-list :

[b]digikam-devel at kde dot org[/b]

To help digiKam project, you can [u]donate money to developers[/u]. Take a look at this page for details:

[url]http://www.digikam.org/donation[/url]
[read more]
item.description:
digiKam
(Graphic Apps)
digiKam is an advanced digital [b]photo management application[/b], which makes importing and organizing digital photos a "snap". The photos can be organized in albums which can be sorted chronologically, by directory layout or by custom collections.
Tired of the directory constraints? Don’t worry, digiKam also provides [b]tagging functionality[/b]. You tag your images which can be spread out across multiple folders and digiKam provides fast and intuitive ways to browse these tagged images. You can also add [b]comments and rating[/b] to your images. digiKam makes use of a fast and robust database to store these meta-informations which makes adding and editing of comments and tags very reliable.

An easy to use interface is provided that enables you to [b]connect to your camera[/b] and preview, download and/or delete your images. digiKam includes too an image editor for image corrections and manipulations.

Another tool, which most artists and photographers will be familiar with, is a [b]Light Table[/b]. This tool assists artists and photographers with reviewing their work ensuring only the highest quality. A light table will show the artist their imperfections in which they can go and touch up. Well in digiKam, the light table function provides the user a similar experience. You can import a photo, drag it onto the light table, and touch up only the areas that need it.

The digiKam [b]Image Editor[/b] come with some common tools e.g. red eye correction or colors correction. Additional tools are provided with the main application to process advanced corrections on image like color management, noise reduction, or special effects. digiKam Image Editor support all camera RAW file formats, 16 bits color depth, Exif/Makernote/IPTC/GPS metadata, Color management, tagging/rating/comments pictures, etc.

A lots of search tools are also available to be able to find specific photo on whole collection : [b]Time line[/b], Calendar, Advanced Search using multiple and complex database queries (tags, rating, keywords, date, location, camera, shot settings, etc.)

A stand-alone image editor version named [b]Showfoto[/b] is also available. It runs without digiKam images database support, but provides all Image Editor tools.

See the related project page for more information:

[url]http://www.digikam.org[/url]

Online documentations:

[url]http://docs.kde.org/development/en/extragear-graphics/digikam/index.html[/url]
[url]http://docs.kde.org/development/en/extragear-graphics/showfoto/index.html[/url]

NOTE:

- Please, use the digiKam mailing-list for all comments:

[b]digikam-users at kde dot org[/b]

- Please, use Bug Tracking System for wishes and reports:
[url]http://bugs.kde.org/buglist.cgi?product=digikam[/url]

You can contact developers on Freenode IRC channels:

[b]#digikam[/b]

or using the developers mailing-list :

[b]digikam-devel at kde dot org[/b]

To help digiKam project, you can [u]donate money to developers[/u]. Take a look at this page for details:

[url]http://www.digikam.org/donation[/url]
[read more]
item.content:encoded:
digiKam
(Graphic Apps)
digiKam is an advanced digital [b]photo management application[/b], which makes importing and organizing digital photos a "snap". The photos can be organized in albums which can be sorted chronologically, by directory layout or by custom collections.
Tired of the directory constraints? Don’t worry, digiKam also provides [b]tagging functionality[/b]. You tag your images which can be spread out across multiple folders and digiKam provides fast and intuitive ways to browse these tagged images. You can also add [b]comments and rating[/b] to your images. digiKam makes use of a fast and robust database to store these meta-informations which makes adding and editing of comments and tags very reliable.

An easy to use interface is provided that enables you to [b]connect to your camera[/b] and preview, download and/or delete your images. digiKam includes too an image editor for image corrections and manipulations.

Another tool, which most artists and photographers will be familiar with, is a [b]Light Table[/b]. This tool assists artists and photographers with reviewing their work ensuring only the highest quality. A light table will show the artist their imperfections in which they can go and touch up. Well in digiKam, the light table function provides the user a similar experience. You can import a photo, drag it onto the light table, and touch up only the areas that need it.

The digiKam [b]Image Editor[/b] come with some common tools e.g. red eye correction or colors correction. Additional tools are provided with the main application to process advanced corrections on image like color management, noise reduction, or special effects. digiKam Image Editor support all camera RAW file formats, 16 bits color depth, Exif/Makernote/IPTC/GPS metadata, Color management, tagging/rating/comments pictures, etc.

A lots of search tools are also available to be able to find specific photo on whole collection : [b]Time line[/b], Calendar, Advanced Search using multiple and complex database queries (tags, rating, keywords, date, location, camera, shot settings, etc.)

A stand-alone image editor version named [b]Showfoto[/b] is also available. It runs without digiKam images database support, but provides all Image Editor tools.

See the related project page for more information:

[url]http://www.digikam.org[/url]

Online documentations:

[url]http://docs.kde.org/development/en/extragear-graphics/digikam/index.html[/url]
[url]http://docs.kde.org/development/en/extragear-graphics/showfoto/index.html[/url]

NOTE:

- Please, use the digiKam mailing-list for all comments:

[b]digikam-users at kde dot org[/b]

- Please, use Bug Tracking System for wishes and reports:
[url]http://bugs.kde.org/buglist.cgi?product=digikam[/url]

You can contact developers on Freenode IRC channels:

[b]#digikam[/b]

or using the developers mailing-list :

[b]digikam-devel at kde dot org[/b]

To help digiKam project, you can [u]donate money to developers[/u]. Take a look at this page for details:

[url]http://www.digikam.org/donation[/url]
[read more]

item.title: Click Radio [Audioplayers]
item.itunes:title: Click Radio [Audioplayers]
item.itunes:author: Upgreyed
item.itunes:summary:
Click Radio
(Audioplayers)
Click Radio is lightweight and simple gui interface media app
project of mine as to be very easy to use.
Click and play features:
* Music radio streamer
* Streamripper recorder
* Plays local music files
* Creates a music playlist
* CD-ROM music player
* CD-R music burner
* Youtube media downloader

Tested and works on MATE, Cinnamon, Budgie, KDE Plasma, and XFCE desktop
Will not work on or show notification menu on Gnome3 desktop

[read more]
item.description:
Click Radio
(Audioplayers)
Click Radio is lightweight and simple gui interface media app
project of mine as to be very easy to use.
Click and play features:
* Music radio streamer
* Streamripper recorder
* Plays local music files
* Creates a music playlist
* CD-ROM music player
* CD-R music burner
* Youtube media downloader

Tested and works on MATE, Cinnamon, Budgie, KDE Plasma, and XFCE desktop
Will not work on or show notification menu on Gnome3 desktop

[read more]
item.content:encoded:
Click Radio
(Audioplayers)
Click Radio is lightweight and simple gui interface media app
project of mine as to be very easy to use.
Click and play features:
* Music radio streamer
* Streamripper recorder
* Plays local music files
* Creates a music playlist
* CD-ROM music player
* CD-R music burner
* Youtube media downloader

Tested and works on MATE, Cinnamon, Budgie, KDE Plasma, and XFCE desktop
Will not work on or show notification menu on Gnome3 desktop

[read more]

item.title: Principia [Various Games]
item.itunes:title: Principia [Various Games]
item.itunes:author: rollerozxa
item.itunes:summary:
Principia
(Various Games)
Principia is a physics-based sandbox building game. Build and play advanced contraptions, circuiting, minigames, robot adventures and more.
The sandbox contains 200+ different objects ranging from basic wooden building blocks to circuitry, electrical and mechanical components, small robots you can control and fight against, and a LuaScript object featuring Lua scripting that can interact with the world. Build a controllable car, a calculator or make your own game with circuitry or in Lua.

The Principia community site contains tens of thousands of creative levels made by players like you, from impressive contraptions to amusing games all for you to browse through and play. If something really peaks your interest, open the level in the sandbox to see how it ticks. What will you create in the sandbox?

Principia started out as a paid proprietary game developed during 2013-2014 before being abandoned due to financial issues. Now the game is an open source project since 2022 developed by the same community who fell in love with it back in the day, free for anyone to play well into the future.
[read more]
item.description:
Principia
(Various Games)
Principia is a physics-based sandbox building game. Build and play advanced contraptions, circuiting, minigames, robot adventures and more.
The sandbox contains 200+ different objects ranging from basic wooden building blocks to circuitry, electrical and mechanical components, small robots you can control and fight against, and a LuaScript object featuring Lua scripting that can interact with the world. Build a controllable car, a calculator or make your own game with circuitry or in Lua.

The Principia community site contains tens of thousands of creative levels made by players like you, from impressive contraptions to amusing games all for you to browse through and play. If something really peaks your interest, open the level in the sandbox to see how it ticks. What will you create in the sandbox?

Principia started out as a paid proprietary game developed during 2013-2014 before being abandoned due to financial issues. Now the game is an open source project since 2022 developed by the same community who fell in love with it back in the day, free for anyone to play well into the future.
[read more]
item.content:encoded:
Principia
(Various Games)
Principia is a physics-based sandbox building game. Build and play advanced contraptions, circuiting, minigames, robot adventures and more.
The sandbox contains 200+ different objects ranging from basic wooden building blocks to circuitry, electrical and mechanical components, small robots you can control and fight against, and a LuaScript object featuring Lua scripting that can interact with the world. Build a controllable car, a calculator or make your own game with circuitry or in Lua.

The Principia community site contains tens of thousands of creative levels made by players like you, from impressive contraptions to amusing games all for you to browse through and play. If something really peaks your interest, open the level in the sandbox to see how it ticks. What will you create in the sandbox?

Principia started out as a paid proprietary game developed during 2013-2014 before being abandoned due to financial issues. Now the game is an open source project since 2022 developed by the same community who fell in love with it back in the day, free for anyone to play well into the future.
[read more]
